Specifically: PPA_Business_Questionaire.xlsx - Business Questionnaire On sheet “ Business Questionnaire Reqs ” you will find: Functional / Business requirements related to both Digitalization and AI Functionalities across the entire Procurement lifecycle. opp.ais_why: Their upper management wants to make the organization future ready and hence a key need for this project is for the vendor to have AI capabilities. AI is a very important theme for them because they are looking for faster, more efficient, and auditable procurement processes. Their current setup is manual, with Excel and email, so AI can help reduce manual intervention, improve reporting, and support better decision-making. They are specifically interested in AI capabilities that can support procurement digitalization, sourcing, supplier management, and future contract-related processes.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The two mapped contacts are Pantelis Kazatzis (Procurement Manager, Evaluator) and Achilleas Spanos (IT Business Analyst, Evaluator), both operational stakeholders. Neither has engaged with Zycus since the opportunity was created 16 June 2026. Zycus initiated outreach on 30 June with a comprehensive questionnaire requesting responses by 10 July, but no reply has been received as of today (1 July). There is no evidence of advocacy (no introductions, no shared internal docs, no pushing back on competitors, no driving the agenda on Zycus's behalf). Access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker is unknown because those roles are unmapped. A champion cannot exist without engagement, access, and demonstrated advocacy. The current state is 'potential contact, zero engagement'. The move is to convert Pantelis Kazatzis into a champion by: (1) securing a discovery call within 7 days where Zycus listens to his pain and aligns Zycus's solution to his goals, (2) asking him to introduce Zycus to the CFO or General Manager who controls the budget, and (3) offering a quid-pro-quo value exchange (e.g. share a Greek public-sector procurement best-practices benchmark or a peer reference in exchange for access to the Economic Buyer). If Pantelis does not engage by 10 July, escalate to his manager or pivot to Achilleas Spanos.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been identified from any source. Salesforce Competitors__c is unknown. Zero discovery or demo calls have occurred where the buyer mentioned other vendors. The Next_Step__c (30 June 2026) does not reference competitive activity. The Business and Technical Questionnaire shared by Zycus requests the buyer to confirm functional, non-functional, and AI requirements, which is typical of a multi-vendor RFP preparation process, but no competitor is named. In Greek public sector at €150k, if Piraeus Port Authority is required to run a formal competitive tender under EU procurement rules, competitors will enter through the open RFP (likely SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, Jaggaer, or regional players). The procurement vehicle (sole-source vs open tender) is unconfirmed, so competitive exposure is unknown. If this is sole-source, Zycus may be the only vendor; if it is an open RFP, Zycus is at risk of being benchmarked against incumbents with stronger Greek public-sector references. The wedge is unavailable because the competitive landscape is invisible. The move is to ask on the discovery call: 'Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus, or is this a sole-source evaluation? If you are comparing, who else is in the mix, and what are the top two reasons you are looking beyond them?'

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec) are completely unknown. The Next_Step__c (30 June 2026) mentions that Zycus should 'check implementation support options for Greece, including local integrator or Big Four partner possibilities', which signals awareness that delivery may require a local partner, but this is an internal Zycus action item, not a confirmed buyer requirement. decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are partially visible through the Business and Technical Questionnaire that Zycus shared on 30 June 2026 (Next_Step__c). The questionnaire requests buyer confirmation on functional and business requirements for digitalization and AI across the procurement lifecycle, non-functional requirements, a user/license matrix analysis, and reporting capabilities (Reporting 1, 2, Procurement Plan sheets). The Technical Questionnaire asks for responses on AI architecture and technical capabilities. This signals that the buyer is running a structured evaluation and has prepared a formal requirements matrix, which is typical of public-sector RFP preparation. However, the buyer has not yet returned answers (due 10 July), so Zycus does not yet know which criteria are weighted most heavily, which are must-haves vs nice-to-haves, or how AI/Merlin features rank against competitors. No discovery or demo call has occurred where the buyer articulated their top three evaluation priorities. The partial insight is that criteria exist and are documented by the buyer; the gap is that Zycus has not yet seen them or shaped the evaluation in Zycus's favor. The move is to use the 10 July questionnaire response as a forcing function to schedule a discovery call immediately after submission, where Zycus walks through the buyer's returned matrix and asks: 'Of these 50 requirements, which three are most critical to your CFO or General Manager, and why?'

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — No AIS (AI Score, AI Status, AI Why) data exists in Salesforce. However, the buyer's explicit focus on AI in the Technical Questionnaire (30 June Next_Step__c: 'gain a better understanding of the AI architecture and technical capabilities of your software') and the Business Questionnaire's emphasis on 'AI Functionalities across the entire Procurement lifecycle' signal strong AI appetite. This is an AI Curious to AI Hungry buyer. The questionnaire asks Zycus to detail AI architecture, which implies the buyer is not simply checking a box but evaluating AI as a core capability. Products in scope include Merlin and Merlin Intake, which align with this appetite. Zero calls have occurred to validate whether AI is a strategic priority or a nice-to-have. The move is to position Merlin as the wedge in the discovery call and demo: show agentic procurement in action (Merlin negotiation agent, Merlin intake agent) and ask the buyer to articulate the AI business case they will present to their CFO or General Manager.

Their current setup is manual, with Excel and email, so AI can help reduce manual intervention, improve reporting, and support better decision-making. They are specifically interested in AI capabilities that can support procurement digitalization, sourcing, supplier management, and future contract-related processes.
